actionable_feedback: Add an explicit representative formula (exact formula text) for one derived metric cell in the memo (e.g., show the exact formula used for the first Row_Level_Evidence derived metric).
Create a 'Formula_Audit' sheet in alarm_fatigue_4.xlsx that lists key formula cells and their formula text (or paste formulas as text) for ranges I2:I4, M2:M4, Q2:Q4, U2:U4, AB2:AB4.
Include one small numeric sensitivity example in the memo showing original values, perturbed values, and resulting classification to make the sensitivity-check immediately reproducible for reviewers.
Clarify grader/verifier metadata: if semantic checks were not run in this environment, remove or justify semantic correctness scores in metadata and memo so outputs are internally consistent.
If possible, attach a short screenshot (PNG) of the spreadsheet showing formula text or provide a CSV/plaintext export of formula strings to make verification possible in constrained environments.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: clear and well-structured; contains decision rules, intermediate values, and recommendation table. Add an explicit example formula and a numeric sensitivity worked example.

feedback_summary: Baseline meets the artifact contract and the grader/verifier passed the attempt. Two residual concerns: (1) the evaluation environment could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable), so exact cell-by-cell formula verification was not performed; (2) grader metadata shows an internal inconsistency about whether semantic correctness was evaluated (some fields state 'not evaluated' while semantic scores are present). Provide clearer, verifiable artifacts to remove residual uncertainty.
